attributes.title: Security Analytics — Correlatable Perimeter Block Events
attributes.description: Proxy KPI: count of perimeter block/alert verdicts already in suru-* (event.action:"block" or "blocked", or threat.indicator.* set, or event.kind:"alert") that are the inputs the SIEM correlation engine consumes. This is NOT a count of OpenSearch Security Analytics findings/correlation incidents — that system index has not been live-confirmed (see dashboard description). MITRE: TA0011 Command and Control (T1071), TA0001 Initial Access, TA0043 Reconnaissance (T1590). False positives: CDN/shared-hosting IP blocks and ad-network DNSBL hits inflate this count without indicating compromise.

kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON: {"query": {"query": "event.action:\"block\" or event.action:\"blocked\" or event.kind:\"alert\" or threat.indicator.type:*", "language": "kuery"}, "filter": [], "indexRefName": "kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON.index"}

attributes.visState: {"title": "Security Analytics \u2014 Correlatable Perimeter Block Events", "type": "metric", "aggs": [{"id": "1", "enabled": true, "type": "count", "params": {}, "schema": "metric"}], "params": {"addTooltip": true, "addLegend": false, "type": "metric", "metric": {"percentageMode": false, "useRanges": false, "colorSchema": "Green to Red", "metricColorMode": "None", "colorsRange": [{"from": 0, "to": 10000}], "labels": {"show": true}, "invertColors": false, "style": {"bgFill": "#000", "bgColor": false, "labelColor": false, "subText": "", "fontSize": 30}}}}

attributes.title: Security Analytics — Correlation-Input Events Over Time
attributes.description: Date histogram of the perimeter block/alert events (split by event.module) that feed the SIEM correlation engine's de-dup and chain logic — e.g. the headline pfBlockerNG+Suricata+Zeek C2 chain. MITRE: TA0011 Command and Control (T1071 Application Layer Protocol), TA0043 Reconnaissance (T1590). False positives: benign DNSBL/ad-block hits and routine inline-IPS drops will dominate volume; correlate with rule.category/threat.indicator.type before escalating.

kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON: {"query": {"query": "event.action:\"block\" or event.action:\"blocked\" or event.kind:\"alert\" or threat.indicator.type:*", "language": "kuery"}, "filter": [], "indexRefName": "kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON.index"}

attributes.title: Security Analytics — Top Candidate-Incident Source IPs
attributes.description: Top source.ip values across perimeter block/alert events — the candidate hosts a correlation rule would chain into a single incident (e.g. one host triggering pfBlockerNG + Suricata + Zeek anomalous-DNS in the same window). MITRE: TA0011 Command and Control (T1071), TA0043 Reconnaissance (T1590), TA0010 Exfiltration. False positives: a single noisy internal host (e.g. ad-blocking causing repeat DNSBL hits) can dominate without being compromised — cross-reference with rule.category diversity per host before escalating.

kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON: {"query": {"query": "event.action:\"block\" or event.action:\"blocked\" or event.kind:\"alert\" or threat.indicator.type:*", "language": "kuery"}, "filter": [], "indexRefName": "kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON.index"}

attributes.title: Security Analytics — Correlation Inputs by Source Module
attributes.description: Distribution of correlation-input events by event.module (suricata/pfblockerng/zeek/pfsense) — shows which perimeter sources are feeding the (currently stub-blocked) SIEM correlation engine. MITRE: TA0011 Command and Control (T1071). False positives: pfblockerng/DNSBL volume is typically the largest slice and is mostly benign ad/telemetry blocking.

attributes.title: Security Analytics — Candidate-Incident Host Activity Heat Map
attributes.description: Heat map of top source.ip activity over time across correlation-input events — visual triage aid for spotting a host with sustained multi-event activity, the pattern a correlation rule would chain into one incident. MITRE: TA0011 Command and Control (T1071), TA0043 Reconnaissance (T1590). False positives: a single chatty internal host with routine DNSBL/ad-block hits will show as a hot row without indicating compromise.

attributes.title: Security Analytics — Correlation-Input Event Log (Discover)
attributes.description: Raw event browser for perimeter block/alert events that are the inputs to SIEM correlation rules. This is a defensive fallback view against suru-* (suru-all-index-pattern) — the OpenSearch Security Analytics findings/correlation-incident system index has NOT been live-confirmed for this deployment (see dashboard description and tier3-core/docs/security-analytics.md). MITRE: TA0011 C2, TA0001 Initial Access, TA0043 Reconnaissance.

kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON: {"highlightAll": true, "version": true, "query": {"query": "event.action:\"block\" or event.action:\"blocked\" or event.kind:\"alert\" or threat.indicator.type:*", "language": "kuery"}, "filter": [], "indexRefName": "kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON.index"}

attributes.title: Security Analytics — All Events (Defensive Discover Fallback)
attributes.description: Unfiltered raw event browser across suru-* (all sources). Always safe/non-empty as long as any tier is ingesting, independent of whether Security Analytics detectors/correlation rules are live. Use this panel to confirm ingestion is healthy even while the 5 SURU custom detectors remain [STUB]-blocked on the upstream NPE documented in tier3-core/docs/security-analytics.md. MITRE: TA0011 C2, TA0001 Initial Access, TA0043 Reconnaissance, TA0010 Exfiltration.

attributes.title: SURU — Security Analytics Findings & Correlations
attributes.description: OpenSearch Security Analytics findings/correlation-engine incident view. Sources: suru-* (suru-all-index-pattern) — a DEFENSIVE FALLBACK, not the OpenSearch Security Analytics findings/correlation-incident system index. Per tier3-core/docs/security-analytics.md, the live findings index/endpoint (e.g. a findings system index or _plugins/_security_analytics/findings/_search) was never independently live-probed during T0a/T7, and SURU's 5 custom-rule-bound detectors are currently [STUB]-blocked on an upstream OpenSearch Security Analytics NullPointerException (SigmaRule.fromDict / getRuleFieldMappings) — see that doc's 'Custom Sigma rule creation' section. No live findings or correlated incidents will exist until that bug is resolved and a detector/correlation rule actually fires. This dashboard is built to be valid and non-empty today by showing the perimeter block/alert events (already in suru-*) that ARE the correlation engine's intended inputs — the same headline pfBlockerNG+Suricata+Zeek chain the correlation rules target — so operators can verify ingestion health and triage candidate-incident hosts manually until native SA findings/correlation panels can be added. MITRE: TA0011 Command and Control (T1071 Application Layer Protocol), TA0001 Initial Access, TA0043 Reconnaissance (T1590 Gather Victim Network Information), TA0010 Exfiltration. False positives: CDN/shared-hosting IP blocks, ad-network DNSBL hits, and routine inline-IPS drops dominate volume without indicating compromise — cross-reference rule.category diversity and threat.indicator.type per source.ip before escalating. TODO(when SA findings index is live-confirmed): replace these proxy panels with native findings/correlation-incident panels bound to the confirmed index.
